library.name: Fukuri Work Japanese (WK/JLPT)
library.description: Work-channel phrases & vocab, ranked easiest(1)->hardest by JLPT/grade/WaniKani level; 20 items per level

items.notes: Marcano's signature softener — every request starts with this
Universal softener; Hisayuki uses this constantly
"Just a little"; useful for I'll be a bit late"

items.characters: 今日はそんな感じです
readings: きょうはそんなかんじです
items.primaryReading: きょうはそんなかんじです

items.notes: Shortened かもしれない; Hisayuki's most-used hedge
"Roughly speaking"; very common in dev discussions
Casual: "I'll just go ahead and do it"
"Well then"; transition word for next step
"Tends to become"; 「ビッグバンリリースになりやすい」
"I see / makes sense"; Hisayuki uses this often before pivoting
Casual suffix: 「マージ待ちっぽいです」
"作業が走る" = "the work runs"; sounds odd literally but means "kicks off"
"Time's up"; polite way to wrap things
かな adds uncertainty; softer than plain 思います

readings: よろしくおねがいします

items.notes: Casual thought-trailoff: 〜だなぁって思って

readings: だいじょうぶであれば
items.primaryReading: だいじょうぶであれば

items.notes: Soft "shall we start?"; less assertive than 始めます
Soft request ending: 〜してもらえますか

items.notes: More feeling-heavy than ありがとう
"When you have time"; standard non-urgent request opener
More apologetic than すいません; for actual mistakes
"Just a tiny bit"; Hisayuki: 「ちょこっと実装変えるかも」
Polite form of なるほど
Buzz's go-to soft prompt for input
Verb-stem + やすい = "easy to / prone to"
Standard opener when you're late or finishing setup

items.notes: Casual; ください makes it polite
"ミーティングに参加します" = "I'll join the meeting"

meanings: That kind of feeling / That feeling
items.notes: Buzz's signature ending; "そんな感じです"
"一応共有しときます"; "sharing just in case"
"Almost / mostly"; very common in tech contexts
